title: Xtra Tangy Moroccan Manchego Tagine
description: A tangy and chewy Moroccan dish featuring Manchego cheese, perfect for a weeknight dinner, with a macrobiotic-friendly twist.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in the ground cumin and smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped preserved lemon, chicken broth, and quinoa to the pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Stir in the crumbled Manchego cheese and cook until it is melted and the quinoa is tender.
Season the tagine with salt and pepper to taste, then stir in the chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
